mnemonics: Flip-Flops FLIP state on a CLOCK edge, Latches LATCH data LEVEL-sensitively
keyPoints: Sequential logic circuits have memory and their output depends on both current inputs and previous states.
Flip-flops are edge-triggered (change state only on a clock edge), while latches are level-sensitive (transparent while enable is active).
The basic types of flip-flops are SR, D, JK, and T, each with a specific characteristic table and excitation table.
commonMistakes: Confusing latches (level-sensitive) with flip-flops (edge-triggered) and misapplying them in a design.
Forgetting to consider setup and hold time requirements, which can lead to metastability and unpredictable circuit behavior.
Incorrectly drawing or interpreting state diagrams and forgetting to include all possible state transitions, including self-loops.
